scope.notApplicableTo: All other Chapter 1 topics remain outside this baseline. This is a separate, standalone record — it does not extend, merge with, or modify ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json (scope: ch01-t02/ch01-t03/ch01-t08/ch01-t10) or ARABIC_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, both of which remain entirely separate, unmodified governance records with their own independent version sequences.

filePreservationNote: The approved draft files are unmoved, unrenamed, uncopied, and not promoted into docs/content-design/chapter-01/pilot/. Their internal content and governance fields (blocking, visibility, arabic, etc.) were not altered by this approval. This baseline-approval record, external to the files themselves, is what establishes that the exact versions at the checksums above are approved — mirroring the same pattern already used by ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, which likewise approves the pilot content files in place without relocating or renaming them. The files may continue to be structurally labeled 'draft' internally (generationStatus: "draft-batch1-english-only-generation") even though this external record now marks them baseline-approved.
statementOfApproval: The English-language draft content for ch01-t01 (Fundamental Quantities) and ch01-t04 (Mass, Inertia and Weight), as it exists in the two files listed in approvedDraftFiles at the checksums recorded there, is approved by the project owner as Batch 1 English baseline version 1.0.0. This approval is a project-owner content-readiness decision made on the basis of Claude's completed generation, review, revision, and closure-verification work (see reviewBasis) — it is not an independently derived, third-party human scientific or editorial re-verification beyond that work, and it does not itself authorize Arabic translation, visual production, application integration, or any student-facing use.
approvedCorrectionsNote: Both ch01-corr-001 and ch01-corr-002 carry approvalStatus: "editoriallyApproved" in SCIENTIFIC_CORRECTIONS.json (ch01-corr-002 with a recorded qualification on 'apparent weight' terminology), with approvals.scientificReviewer left null on both by explicit project-owner instruction. This baseline approval relies on, and does not re-open or re-litigate, those already-approved correction records.

nonBlockingConditions: Consider refining the distance wording in ch01-t01-block-equations to emphasize distance traveled where pedagogically useful.
Consider adding a simple numeric illustration of v = d/t in a future revision, if the audited evidence and topic scope come to support one.
Consider refining ch01-t04-block-explanation's 'ordinary situation' phrasing to specify equilibrium or zero net force where context requires more precision.
Add the ch01-term-mass glossary linkage to ch01-t04-block-equations' glossaryTermIds after glossary approval and identifier registration.

nonBlockingConditionsStatement: The conditions listed under perTopicDecision above are optional future improvements only. They do not block this baseline approval and are not represented as unresolved scientific defects — none was found in the final drafting, review, revision, or closure-verification passes (see reviewBasis). They were not applied as edits during this approval task and may be considered in a future, separate, controlled baseline revision, following the exact revisionLog pattern already established by ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json.

governanceRestrictions.approvalLevelStatement: This is a project-owner ENGLISH-CONTENT baseline approval only. It is distinct from: (1) editorial approval of a correction record (a narrower action already recorded separately in SCIENTIFIC_CORRECTIONS.json for ch01-corr-001/ch01-corr-002), (2) independent human scientific review (has not occurred, for any topic, at any point in this project), and (3) student-publication authorization (a separate, still-absent gate).
governanceRestrictions.arabicNotAuthorized: Arabic content is not generated or approved by this record. Both approved draft files' Arabic fields remain arabic.translationStatus: "missing" with null text throughout, unchanged by this approval.
governanceRestrictions.visualProductionNotAuthorized: Visual production is not authorized by this record. ch01-t01-visual-001 and ch01-t04-visual-001 remain unproduced (visualGovernance[0].availabilityStatus: "missing") and provisional.
governanceRestrictions.applicationIntegrationNotAuthorized: Application integration is not authorized by this record. PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json's applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds remains exactly the original four pilot topics, unchanged by this approval.

revisionControlPolicy.rule: Any future edit to the English content of ch01-t01 or ch01-t04 (instructorScript, contentBlock, or problem records in the two Batch 1 draft files) must be recorded as a controlled revision against this baseline, not made silently.
revisionControlPolicy.mechanism: A controlled revision increments baselineVersion (e.g. 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0) and appends an entry to revisionLog below recording what changed, why, who authorized it, and when — following the exact pattern already proven twice in ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json's own revisionControlPolicy.revisionLog.

revisionLog.scope: Adds exactly one new contentBlock record, ch01-t01-block-opening (blockType "openingConcept"), as the first record in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json. No existing record (ch01-is-101, ch01-t01-block-mainidea, ch01-t01-block-explanation, ch01-t01-block-equations, ch01-t01-block-visual, ch01-t01-block-misconception, ch01-t01-block-review) was changed, reordered relative to each other, or removed. No record in ch01-t04 was touched.
revisionLog.whatChanged: A new opening/introductory content block was added ahead of ch01-t01-block-mainidea, built around a source slide ('1.1 Fundamental Physical Quantities') the project owner supplied directly in the implementation task, together with newly authored explanatory prose (three-basic-aspects framing, direct-vs-combined measurements, distance/time/mass/charge as fundamental foundations, derived-quantity examples including a worked v = d / t = 20 m/s car example, a space-vs-distance misconception, a key-concept summary, and a transition to the units-of-measurement topic). The project-owner-supplied source slide's original English wording is preserved verbatim within the new block's text; the surrounding explanatory paragraphs were authored by Claude directly from the project owner's requested structure, not copied from ch01-t01's other existing blocks.
revisionLog.whatWasNotChanged: No instructional prose, equation, visual reference, misconception, or review question in any of ch01-t01's seven pre-existing records was reworded, shortened, reordered relative to one another, or removed. ch01-t01-visual-001.svg was not touched. No record in ch01-t04, and no record in any of the four originally-piloted topics, was changed.
revisionLog.rationale: Requested by the project owner as new opening scientific content for the Fundamental Quantities topic, to be integrated into the existing reading flow without duplicating the wording of the already-approved mainIdea/organizedExplanation/misconception blocks.
resolvedGovernanceRecords: docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json — new record ch01-t01-block-opening appended at index 0
revisionLog.publicationRestrictionsRetained: studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic, including the new record; studentPublicationAuthorized remains false globally; independentExpertReviewCompleted remains false. Unlike the other revisions logged above, this new record has NOT been through the drafting-authorization / generation / review / revision / closure-verification sequence the rest of this baseline's content went through (see reviewBasis) — its blocking.blockingStatus is 'blocked' with blockingReason including 'scientificReviewPending', and it carries no scientificCorrectionIds, reflecting that its scientific accuracy has been checked only by Claude's own authoring process, not by a separate review pass. This revision authorizes the record's presence in the internal-review application build only, on the same terms as every other blocked/draft record in this file — not any elevated review or approval status beyond that.

revisionLog.scope: Strictly limited to ch01-t01-block-opening's localizedContent.en.text (the explanatory prose added after the verbatim source-slide quotation) in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json. The verbatim quoted slide text itself, and every other record in ch01-t01/ch01-t04, is unchanged.
revisionLog.whatChanged: A project-owner scientific/linguistic review of rev-003's explanatory prose (recorded via ch01-english-baseline-rev-003) identified several inaccuracies and revised the wording accordingly: (1) distance/time/mass/charge are now explicitly scoped as 'the quantities this chapter's introductory framework builds its early examples from', not a universal list of every fundamental quantity in physics; (2) the claim that 'every physical quantity...ultimately traces back to' those four was replaced with a context-limited statement; (3) added a new closing 'Scientific note' paragraph stating that in the modern SI system, electric current (not charge) is one of the seven base quantities and charge is derived (Q = I t); (4) 'speed...without needing any unit of its own' was replaced with an explicit statement that speed's derived unit is m/s, formed from distance's and time's own units; (5) 'Saying "far" is not physics; saying "100 m" is' was replaced with a qualitative-vs-quantitative framing; (6) the area/volume/density mentions were trimmed to a brief one-sentence mention (deferred to later sections) rather than developed in this opening slide. The verbatim source-slide quotation at the start of the block is byte-for-byte unchanged from rev-003.
revisionLog.whatWasNotChanged: The verbatim quoted original English slide text ('In physics, there are three basic aspects...distance, time, and two properties of matter called mass and charge.') is unchanged. No instructional prose, equation, visual reference, misconception, or review question in any of ch01-t01's seven pre-existing records was touched. ch01-t01-visual-001.svg was not touched. No record in ch01-t04, and no record in any of the four originally-piloted topics, was changed.
revisionLog.rationale: Project-owner scientific and linguistic review of the draft-ai-generated-unreviewed content added in rev-003 found it presented a textbook-introductory simplification (charge as a 'fundamental' quantity alongside distance/time/mass) as if it were the complete, universal SI base-quantity list, and contained two overstated/imprecise sentences (the 'no unit of its own' claim for speed, and the 'far is not physics' framing). This revision corrects the wording and adds an explicit SI scientific note, without altering the preserved verbatim slide quotation.
revisionLog.publicationRestrictionsRetained: studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic; studentPublicationAuthorized remains false globally; independentExpertReviewCompleted remains false. This record's blocking.blockingStatus remains 'blocked' (blockingReason still includes 'scientificReviewPending') — this revision is the project owner's own review pass on Claude's authored wording, not the independent third-party scientific review the rest of this baseline's content went through, and does not elevate this record to that status.

revisionLog.scope: Strictly limited to ch01-t01-block-opening's localizedContent.en.text (the explanatory prose after the verbatim source-slide quotation) in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json. The verbatim quoted slide text itself, and every other record, is unchanged.
revisionLog.whatChanged: Project-owner pedagogical review of rev-004's wording (now scientifically accurate, per rev-004) found the explanatory prose too long and repetitive for an introductory slide. Restructured into a compact instructional format — Main Idea, five short numbered conceptual steps, Simple Example, Common Misconception, Scientific Note, Connection to the Next Part — replacing the prior sequence of long, loosely-ordered paragraphs. Removed the duplicated explanation of speed's derived unit (m/s, formed from distance's and time's own units), which previously appeared once before the worked example and again after it; the idea is now stated once (in the numbered steps) and the post-example sentence is a short, generic closing ('This example shows how a derived quantity is formed by combining two measured quantities.'). Replaced 'treats distance, time, mass, and charge as the quantities used to describe them in this context' with 'introduces distance, time, mass, and charge as important measurable quantities within its introductory framework'. Replaced 'Distance is the measurable aspect of space.' with 'Distance is one measurable way of describing separation within space.' No scientific idea introduced in rev-004 (the SI base-quantity note, Q = I t, speed's derived unit) was removed — only shortened and de-duplicated.
revisionLog.whatWasNotChanged: The verbatim quoted original English slide text is unchanged. No instructional prose, equation, visual reference, misconception, or review question in any of ch01-t01's seven pre-existing records was touched. ch01-t01-visual-001.svg was not touched. No record in ch01-t04, and no record in any of the four originally-piloted topics, was changed. All scientific corrections from rev-004 (SI base-quantity accuracy, Q = I t, speed's derived unit m/s) are preserved, just stated once instead of twice and more concisely.
revisionLog.rationale: Project-owner pedagogical review found the rev-004 wording, while scientifically accurate, read as too dense and repetitive for a short opening slide, risking making the chapter's first concept feel unnecessarily heavy for a beginning student. This revision compacts the structure and removes the one clear duplication (the derived-unit-of-speed explanation) without cutting any scientific content.

governanceStatusChange.field: ch01-t01-block-opening.arabic.translationStatus (Arabic file only; see ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json revisionLog rev-003 for the paired entry)
governanceStatusChange.note: "draft" is the exact, pre-existing status value this file already uses for every one of ch01-t01's other seven records — all of which also went through this project's own generation/review/revision process without becoming 'independently reviewed' or 'approved' in the stricter sense (see governanceRestrictions.independentExpertReviewCompleted: false, unchanged). It was never the case that 'draft' meant 'unreviewed' in this schema; the custom '-ai-generated-unreviewed' suffix was Claude's own invented marker (added when this record was first authored, with no independent or project-owner review yet performed) and is no longer accurate now that the project owner has directly performed two rounds of scientific and linguistic review on this record's wording (rev-004 and this rev-005). This is NOT equivalent to the independent third-party scientific/linguistic review the rest of this baseline's content went through, and does not change blocking.blockingStatus ('blocked', unchanged), studentFacingAllowed (false, unchanged), or studentPublicationAuthorized (false, unchanged).

revisionLog.publicationRestrictionsRetained: studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic; studentPublicationAuthorized remains false globally; independentExpertReviewCompleted remains false. This record's blocking.blockingStatus remains 'blocked' (blockingReason still includes 'scientificReviewPending') despite the translationStatus wording change described above — that change reflects the project owner's own direct review, not the independent third-party review this baseline's other content went through, and does not itself lift the block.

identifierRegistrationStatus.statement: Identifier registration in IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json is NOT a prerequisite for this internal English baseline approval and was NOT performed as part of it. Registration is required before any future application-integration step. IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json was not modified by this approval.

downstreamStatus.applicationIntegrationStatus: notAuthorized; also not mechanically possible today, since docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ is outside apps/chapter1-mvp/'s current vite.config.ts server.fs.allow / rawImports.ts import scope

unchangedByThisApproval: Every instructorScript, contentBlock, and problem record in the two Batch 1 draft files remains in its existing draft/blocked governance state — blocking.blockingStatus, blocking.studentFacingAllowed, arabic.translationStatus, and every other governance/localization field on each record are untouched by this approval.
The two draft files themselves are unmoved, unrenamed, uncopied, unmodified, and remain at docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/.
ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json and ARABIC_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json remain entirely separate and unmodified by this record.
PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json's scope.authorizedTopicIds and applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds remain exactly the original four pilot topics.
studentFacingAllowed remains false on every record in every topic.
canonicalGenerationAuthorized remains chapter-wide false in PILOT_READINESS.json.
This approval does not authorize Arabic translation, visual production, or application integration for ch01-t01 or ch01-t04.
